title: Structure Control
description: Gives you control over which portals or structures are in your mod pack! Allows you to create a truly themed mod pack. No ruined portals, nether dimension,  end dimensions, or even villages if you so desire.  You're in total control!
body:
![Ohayden's](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4f4c0fab20f2bd4932b2bcbe3116e799b31b55f2.png) ![Structure Control](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/aa6f80df9e9b71bd52e363b3042f08b1f5a18d31.png) Structure Control is a configurable mod to allow any modpack developer to enable or disable various options for portals and structure generation. It allows you to create a more immersive experience when creating a themed mod pack. Suppose you don't want ruined portals since they just don't fit your theme; then you can remove them. Don't want villages? No problem, you can disable them as well. You have total control! Modded structures are even under your control. Would you want to add a few more mods? Are the structures added by those new mods just not fitting in with your theme? With Structure Control, you can disable those too! You can enable or disable the following options as you see fit:
Show Options ---
General Options
--- - Throwing an eye of ender - Using an eye of ender on end portal frames - Nether portal activation - Nether portal activation blocks ---
World Generation
--- - Stronghold - Ruined Portals - Buried Treasure - Desert Pyramid - End City - Nether Fortress - Igloo - Jungle Temple - Mineshaft - Nether Fossil - Ocean Monument - Ocean Ruin - Shipwreck - Swap Hut - Woodland Mansion - Ancient City - Bastion Remnant - Mansion - Pillager Outpost - Trail Ruins - Trial Chambers - Villages - Any other modded structure!
---
Commands
--- Lists all structures found at current location
```/structurecontrol admin find``` Force config reload ```/structurecontrol admin reload``` Displays current version ```/structurecontrol ver``` ---
FAQ
--- > Can I include this mod into my modpack? > > Absolutely! > Will you support X.XX.X version? > > Within reason, we're trying to support the most common versions of Minecraft. > Which mod loaders do you support? > > We currently support Fabric, Neoforge, and Forge. > Where can I report a bug or get support? > > Join my Discord. > > How can I support you? > > Coffee is always nice! >
